core.label: Haskell Fundamentals
core.description: Setup, types, functions, and pure functional basics.

topics.label: GHCup & Stack
topics.description: Install GHC, Cabal, and Stack with GHCup. Stack manages project builds and dependencies.

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: Int, Integer, Double, Char, String, Bool. Haskell is statically typed with powerful type inference.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Define functions with equations, pattern matching, and guards. All functions are curried by default.

topics.label: Lists
topics.description: Cons (:), list comprehensions, head, tail, map, filter, foldl, foldr. Lists are the fundamental data structure.

topics.label: Tuples
topics.description: Fixed-size heterogeneous containers. fst, snd for pairs, and pattern matching for larger tuples.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Decompose values in function definitions, case expressions, and let/where bindings.

topics.label: Guards & Where/Let
topics.description: Conditional logic with guards (|) and local definitions with where and let...in.

topics.label: Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: map, filter, fold, (.), ($), flip. Higher-order functions are the core of Haskell programming.

topics.label: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Anonymous functions with \x -> body. Used for short inline function definitions.

topics.label: Recursion
topics.description: Haskell has no loops — recursion replaces iteration. Understand tail recursion and accumulator patterns.

core.label: Type System & Type Classes
core.description: Algebraic data types, type classes, and polymorphism.

topics.label: Type Signatures
topics.description: Annotate functions with :: for documentation and type checking. Read type signatures fluently.

topics.label: Algebraic Data Types
topics.description: Define custom types with data — sum types (|) and product types. ADTs model domain data precisely.

topics.label: Record Syntax
topics.description: Named fields for data types. Records auto-generate accessor functions.

topics.label: Type Classes
topics.description: Eq, Ord, Show, Read, Num, Functor. Type classes are Haskell's mechanism for ad-hoc polymorphism.

topics.label: Deriving
topics.description: Auto-derive type class instances with deriving. Reduces boilerplate for common instances.

topics.label: Maybe & Either
topics.description: Maybe for optional values, Either for error handling. The functional alternatives to null and exceptions.

topics.label: Parametric Polymorphism
topics.description: Generic functions with type variables (a, b). Haskell's parametricity provides strong guarantees.

topics.label: Newtype & Type Aliases
topics.description: type for aliases, newtype for zero-cost wrappers, data for new types. Choose the right abstraction.

core.label: Functors, Monads & IO
core.description: Functor, Applicative, Monad, and performing I/O.

topics.label: Functor
topics.description: Map over wrapped values with fmap / <$>. Functor is the simplest mapping abstraction.

topics.label: Applicative
topics.description: Apply wrapped functions to wrapped values with <*> and pure. Between Functor and Monad.

topics.label: Monad
topics.description: Sequence computations with >>= (bind) and do-notation. Monads are Haskell's core abstraction for effects.

topics.label: do-Notation
topics.description: Syntactic sugar for monadic code. Makes sequential Monad operations readable.

topics.label: IO Monad
topics.description: Perform side effects (console, files, network) inside the IO monad. Pure code stays separate.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with readFile, writeFile, and handles. All I/O happens in the IO monad.

topics.label: Monad Transformers
topics.description: Stack monads with ReaderT, StateT, ExceptT. Transformers combine multiple effects.

topics.label: Laziness
topics.description: Haskell is lazy by default — expressions are evaluated only when needed. Understand thunks and seq.

core.label: Modules, Testing & Tooling
core.description: Project structure, testing, and the Haskell ecosystem.

topics.label: Modules & Imports
topics.description: Organize code with module declarations and qualified imports.

topics.label: Cabal & Stack Projects
topics.description: Create projects with cabal init or stack new. Manage dependencies in .cabal files.

topics.label: HSpec
topics.description: BDD-style testing with describe, it, shouldBe. The most popular Haskell testing framework.

topics.label: QuickCheck
topics.description: Property-based testing with random input generation. QuickCheck is Haskell's most influential testing tool.

topics.label: Hackage & Hoogle
topics.description: Find packages on Hackage and search by type signature with Hoogle.

topics.label: Common Libraries
topics.description: text, bytestring, containers, aeson (JSON), http-client. The essential Haskell packages.

topics.label: GHC Extensions
topics.description: OverloadedStrings, DeriveGeneric, TypeApplications, GADTs. GHC extensions extend the language.

topics.label: Performance & Profiling
topics.description: Strictness annotations (!), BangPatterns, and GHC profiling. Understand space leaks from laziness.
